task.json metadata and setup_task.sh lines 24-25; export_result.sh line 86
task.json metadata and setup_task.sh line 23; export_result.sh line 68; verifier.py line 68

Direct database query verification: sqlite3 query on customers and invoices tables confirms all columns exist; export_result.sh line 35 checks for customerid, export_result.sh line 42 checks for lastname and spend

Direct database query: SELECT customers.CustomerId, FirstName, LastName, Email, SUM(invoices.Total) as TotalSpend FROM customers INNER JOIN invoices ON customers.CustomerId = invoices.CustomerId GROUP BY customers.CustomerId ORDER BY TotalSpend DESC LIMIT 10 returns Customer ID 6 (Helena Holý) as top spender with 49.62 total spend
Direct database query verification: Customer ID 6 (Helena Holý) has total lifetime spend of 49.62 across all invoices; confirmed via sqlite3 query

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the Chinook database, a real public sample database originally from GitHub (https://github.com/lerocha/chinook-database) containing digital media store data based on iTunes Library. All metadata claims are verified against the actual database: Customer ID 6 (Helena Holý with email hholy@gmail.com) is the top spender with a total lifetime spend of 49.62. The required query columns (CustomerId, FirstName, LastName, Email, TotalSpend) all exist in the customers and invoices tables. The expected output path is /home/ga/Documents/exports/top_spenders.csv, and the DBeaver task should be named WeeklyTopSpenders.
